# GoalOS Ascension Inflow Control Room v21

The Ascension Inflow Control Room is a browser-local public demonstration of the open-system GoalOS/AGI ALPHA control idea.

It demonstrates that the system does not improve by maximizing all resources or all autonomy. It improves when inflows are regulated through proof gates:

```text
Compute + Data + Tasks + Incentives + Feedback + Governance + Tools
→ proof-gated verified work
→ Chronicle memory
→ reusable capability
```

The page lets users tune seven inflows and observe the resulting regime:

- `SUSTAINED_ASCENSION`
- `INERT_QUEUE`
- `STALE_SELF_REFERENCE`
- `UNGOVERNED_ACTUATION`
- `CHAOTIC_SWARM`
- `BUREAUCRATIC_STALL`
- `NEEDS_REBALANCE`

The page exports a public-safe Evidence Docket JSON with no account, no cookies, no analytics, no storage, no wallet, no network request, no token route, no transaction broadcast, no production authority, and no user data wanted.

## Why this page exists

Prior public demos show:

- Proof-to-Action Theatre: objective → proof → governed decision state.
- Proof Gradient Arena: candidate → hard gates → promotion or rejection.
- Chronicle Compounding Lab: accepted proof → memory → reusable capability.

This v21 page adds the open-system layer: regulated inflow is what keeps the institution from becoming idle, stale, chaotic, or over-governed.

## Claim boundary

This is a public-safe browser demonstration. It is not factual certification, external audit, investment advice, legal advice, Mainnet operation, or production authority.
